behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: This tests request construction logic that is local to the module. A mock HTTP client can capture and inspect the request body to verify all labels are included. The failure (forgetting to include labels) has no I/O or OS dependency—it's a pure logic bug in request building. No real network or external service is needed to catch it.
behaviors.test_description: Set up the classification module with a known taxonomy (e.g., ['legal', 'technical', 'financial', 'other']). Mock the external API HTTP client to capture the outgoing request. Call classify() with a test document. Assert that the request body contains all expected taxonomy labels as a complete list. Verify the labels are present in the correct serialization format before any HTTP transmission.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns 'unknown' instead of forwarding the unexpected value.
behaviors.justification: This tests response validation and sanitization—local logic that filters and normalizes API output. Mock the API to return an invalid label (e.g., 'spam' when taxonomy is ['legal', 'technical', 'financial', 'other']). Verify the module returns 'unknown'. No I/O or OS dependency—this is pure data transformation.
behaviors.test_description: Set up the classification module with a fixed taxonomy. Mock the external API to return a label not in the taxonomy. Call classify() with a test document. Assert that the returned classification is 'unknown', not the API's invalid response. Test with multiple invalid values (null, numeric strings, random text) to verify robust sanitization.

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: This most likely tests caching behavior (not LLM determinism, which would require real API calls). The failure is 'module doesn't cache, so it calls the API twice for the same document.' Mock the API and track call count; verify it's called exactly once on the first call and the second call is served from cache. The mock is sufficient because we are testing the caching mechanism, not LLM consistency. SELF-CHECK: The test must explicitly verify API call count to avoid false positives.
behaviors.test_description: Set up the classification module. Mock the external API and instrument it to track the number of calls. Call classify() with the same test document twice. Assert that both calls return identical results. Assert that the mocked API was called exactly once (second call hit cache). Test with different documents to verify cache is document-scoped, not global.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: A mocked token counter would trivially return predictable values, hiding real token overages. REAL-INFRA: Must use a real tokenizer (e.g., tiktoken) to verify actual token consumption on realistic documents. A mock defeats the test's purpose entirely. The failure 'request exceeded token budget' is invisible to a mocked token counter. Requires real tokenization library, but still local (no external API needed).
behaviors.test_description: Set up the classification module with a per-call budget (e.g., 500 tokens). Prepare a representative test document of typical size. Integrate a real tokenizer (tiktoken or equivalent for the target model). Call classify() and intercept the request. Count tokens in the actual request using the real tokenizer. Assert token count ≤ budget. Test edge cases: long documents, special characters, multilingual text to ensure consistent behavior.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This tests the module's retry and error-handling logic. Mock the API to return a rate-limit signal (e.g., HTTP 429) on the first call and success on the second. Mock the sleep/wait function to avoid real delays. Verify the final result is the successful response, not the error. MOCK-MISS: No real network needed—the failure 'module returns error to caller' is caught by mocking.
behaviors.test_description: Set up the classification module. Mock the external API to return HTTP 429 on first call, then succeed on second call. Mock the sleep/wait function to return immediately (no test delay). Call classify() with a test document. Assert the final result is a successful classification, not a rate-limit error. Assert the API was called exactly twice. Assert sleep was invoked between retry attempts.
